agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that applies bulk discount logic and enforces a $0.01 price floor. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak). No external dependencies, no I/O — pure, stateless, deterministic.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any inputs, recalculatePrice(...) >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 100) === 90 AND recalculatePrice(9, 100) === 100"},{"rule":"The $0.01 floor takes precedence over any discount calculation.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 0.05) === 0.01 (because 0.05 - 10% = 0.045, floored to 0.01)"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function, no classes or external dependencies","implementation_note":"Single exported function: recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number. Stateless and deterministic — same inputs always produce same output."},{"decision":"Inline execution, no async","implementation_note":"Called synchronously from a hot path at 500 req/sec. No Promises, no I/O. Keep the function body minimal."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price of $0.01 can appear. Document the floor rule directly above the function signature."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below quantity threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced when discount would zero out price","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"},{"criterion":"Unit test suite covers all cases","given":"the implemented function","when":"tests are run","then":"all three acceptance criteria above are covered, plus edge cases (quantity=0, basePrice=0.01, large quantities)"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier applied when quantity >= 10","definition":"A single discount tier: if quantity is 10 or more, reduce price by 10%. No other discount tiers exist currently."},{"domain_term":"Price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(computedPrice, 0.01)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price is $0.01. Applied as the last step after all discounts."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are explicit and unambiguous","All three agent outputs are fully aligned","Acceptance criteria are concrete with exact expected values"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether future discount tiers beyond the single 10% bulk tier are anticipated (not mentioned by any agent)","Floating-point rounding strategy not specified — Shaq should decide whether to round to 2 decimal places"],"assumptions":["Only one discount tier exists (10% at quantity >= 10). No stacking or combinatorial discounts.","basePrice is provided in dollars with up to 2 decimal places","quantity is a positive integer","Rounding to 2 decimal places (cents) is expected for currency output"]}}

content.thinking: Produce a handoff brief for Shaq based on Bird's domain analysis, MJ's architecture summary, and Pippen's operational note.

content.text: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that applies bulk discount logic and enforces a $0.01 price floor. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak). No external dependencies, no I/O — pure, stateless, deterministic.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any inputs, recalculatePrice(...) >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 100) === 90 AND recalculatePrice(9, 100) === 100"},{"rule":"The $0.01 floor takes precedence over any discount calculation.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 0.05) === 0.01 (because 0.05 - 10% = 0.045, floored to 0.01)"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function, no classes or external dependencies","implementation_note":"Single exported function: recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number. Stateless and deterministic — same inputs always produce same output."},{"decision":"Inline execution, no async","implementation_note":"Called synchronously from a hot path at 500 req/sec. No Promises, no I/O. Keep the function body minimal."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price of $0.01 can appear. Document the floor rule directly above the function signature."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below quantity threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced when discount would zero out price","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"},{"criterion":"Unit test suite covers all cases","given":"the implemented function","when":"tests are run","then":"all three acceptance criteria above are covered, plus edge cases (quantity=0, basePrice=0.01, large quantities)"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier applied when quantity >= 10","definition":"A single discount tier: if quantity is 10 or more, reduce price by 10%. No other discount tiers exist currently."},{"domain_term":"Price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(computedPrice, 0.01)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price is $0.01. Applied as the last step after all discounts."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are explicit and unambiguous","All three agent outputs are fully aligned","Acceptance criteria are concrete with exact expected values"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether future discount tiers beyond the single 10% bulk tier are anticipated (not mentioned by any agent)","Floating-point rounding strategy not specified — Shaq should decide whether to round to 2 decimal places"],"assumptions":["Only one discount tier exists (10% at quantity >= 10). No stacking or combinatorial discounts.","basePrice is provided in dollars with up to 2 decimal places","quantity is a positive integer","Rounding to 2 decimal places (cents) is expected for currency output"]}}
